steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Quantitative) question. It asks for a specific data point: the CFU count in the venom-treated chamber for petri dish 6. The task is to locate row 6 in the table and read the venom column value — no inference required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 153: this is the value in the venom-treated column for petri dish 6. Reading directly from that cell confirms 153 CFUs. ✓
  • Choice B — 977: this value appears in the saline-treated column for petri dish 6, not the venom column. It is the wrong chamber's reading for the same dish. ✗
  • Choice C — 55: this value comes from a different petri dish's venom column, not dish 6. Misreading the row produces this error. ✗
  • Choice D — 103: this value also belongs to a different row's venom column. It does not correspond to petri dish 6. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
